_note: Manual overrides for GA legislative race candidates, applied after build_legislative_races.py generates from source data. Keys are candidate IDs (e.g. 'ga-house-1-2026-r-1'). Use _name for human-readability only — it is stripped before merging. Fields here override the auto-generated values. Incumbents get imageUrl auto-populated from ga-members.json; use this file to correct bad source data or add fields for challengers.

_raceOverrides._note: Race-level field overrides keyed by race ID. Applied by build_legislative_races.py after building each race. Use '_note' to set the Open Seat banner text (format: 'Open seat — Reason.').

ga-senate-7-2026._note: Open seat — Nabilah Parkes (D) resigned effective March 13, 2026.

ga-senate-12-2026._note: Open seat — Freddie Powell Sims (D) resigned effective March 23, 2026.

ga-senate-19-2026._note: Open seat — Blake Tillery (R) vacated to run for Lt. Governor.

ga-house-177-2026._note: Open seat — Dexter Sharper (D) resigned effective March 9, 2026.

ga-senate-15-2026._note: Open Seat — Ed Harbison (D) announced March 4, 2026 he would not seek re-election.

ga-house-1-2026-r-1.bio: In 2020 Mike was elected as State Representative for Georgia House District 1, which includes parts of Walker and all of Dade Counties. In 2022 and 2024, Mike was reelected as your State Representative.

ga-house-113-2026-d-2.bio: As your State Representative, I will be a progressive voice in the Georgia State House and a present, accountable partner here at home. I will work to lower the cost of living, expand access to quality and affordable healthcare, strengthen our public schools, and hold powerful corporations accountable when they put profits over people. You will see me at the grocery store, the park, church, neighborhood meetings, and local cookouts; listening, learning, and serving. Representation does not stop in Atlanta; it begins and ends in the community. In the spirit of my father's legacy, and guided by the values he passed down to me, I am proud to announce my candidacy for the Georgia House of Representatives in District 113.

ga-house-113-2026-d-1.bio: I have served as a local elected official where I have written resolutions and championed community priorities. As a lobbyist for the State of Georgia, I have written bills and testified on various issues affecting our communities. My work with Newton County Commission demonstrated my commitment to delivering results and understanding how to navigate the legislative process effectively. I believe in concrete action and will consistently report on the progress and outcomes of my efforts, ensuring transparency and trust.

ga-house-114-2026-d-1.bio: I am Michael Caw, a retired research and development technician, community volunteer, and proud resident of Newborn, Georgia. I am running for the Georgia House of Representatives to bring thoughtful, data-driven leadership and a neighbor's common sense to the State Capitol.

ga-house-114-2026-r-1.bio: Georgia might be ranked the number one state to do business, and that's great if you're a large corporation, but I'm focused on making Georgia the best place in the country to own and grow a small business. That means cutting red tape, making essential agencies more efficient, and pushing bold initiatives like eliminating the state income tax. Now is not the time for conservatives to be timid. We need leaders in Atlanta with real world experience who will fight for working families and main street—not just the big guys.

ga-house-115-2026-r-1.bio: I'm running to ensure that responsible growth in our communities benefits residents, not just developers; that homeowners aren't priced out of their own neighborhoods; that our infrastructure, schools, and public safety measures keep pace with the growth; and that the needs of seniors and working families are respected and not overlooked. I believe that government should be accountable, practical, and deeply rooted in the real lives of the people it represents. My goal is straightforward: to be a voice you recognize, a leader you can reach, and a representative who shows up and gets the important work done.

ga-house-115-2026-d-1.bio: Elected to the Georgia General Assembly in 2020, Lewis-Ward partnered with the Gordon State College Foundation to establish an empowerment scholarship for women interested in American politics, aiming to address the under-representation of African American women in public office. In 2025, she established a second scholarship at Clayton State University to inspire future leaders in public service. She also secured a grant through a program administered by the GA Forestry Commission to increase urban tree canopy, directing resources to disadvantaged communities across her legislative district. Lewis-Ward helped secure almost $400,000 in the FY 2022 state budget for students in her district to participate in the American Association of Adapted Sports Program, and $39,000 for the startup of a Court Appointed Special Advocate (CASA) program in Henry County, GA. She also co-sponsored bipartisan legislation, 2021 HB 676, now signed into law, that provides legislative advisory oversight of state farmers' markets. In 2023, the House of Representatives passed HB 144, the George Burgess Act, introduced by Representative Lewis-Ward to strengthen the rights of adult wards under guardianship.

ga-senate-42-2026-r-1.bio: Grounded in faith, guided by the Constitution, and driven by a commitment to serve others, Bret is ready to lead with bold conservative leadership. He will bring the same discipline, grit, and determination he learned in the Marine Corps to the fight for faith, family, and freedom. Our families do not need more politics as usual, we need a conservative fighter. Bret Dunn is ready to bring bold, principled and unapologetic conservative leadership to the Senate. He will stand tall to fight for our values and deliver real conservative results.

ga-senate-43-2026-d-2.bio: Formerly House Representative from District 113, she was suspended from her seat by Gov. Kemp due to her ongoing criminal case.

ga-senate-43-2026-d-3.bio: Senator Tonya P. Anderson, a Democrat, has served in the Georgia State Senate since 2016. She represents Senate District 43, which includes Rockdale County and portions of Dekalb, Gwinnett and Newton counties. Prior to serving as State Senator, she served as the 1st State Representative for House District 92. Prior to serving as a State Representative, she served as City Councilwoman and Mayor for the City of Lithonia.

ga-house-118-2026-d-1.bio: I'm a Newton County homeowner, a mother, and a small business owner since 2012. I built my career by solving real problems for real people—helping organizations grow, leading strategy, and delivering outcomes. Now, I'm bringing that same results-driven approach to the State House—focused on what families in District 118 are living every day: cost of living pressures, school and youth outcomes, public safety, and healthcare.

ga-house-118-2026-d-2.bio: I'm stepping forward not just as a candidate—but as someone who believes in the power of change. For too long, many in our communities have felt unheard and unseen. I'm stepping forward because I believe our district deserves leadership that uplifts families, strengthens, protects our rights and stands firmly for justice for all. This moment is about rising together. It's about turning our challenges into purpose and our hopes into real progress for the people who call District 118 home. When we stand together, we can build a future where every voice matters and every family has the opportunity to thrive. I'm honored to take this step with you.

ga-house-118-2026-r-1.bio: State Representative Clint Crowe represents the citizens of House District 118, which includes Butts County, as well as portions of Monroe and Newton counties. He was elected to the Georgia House of Representatives in 2020. Rep. Crowe is a second-generation realtor, and he has worked in the real estate industry for more than 20 years. Prior to running a real estate business, Rep. Crowe had a decade long career in law enforcement as a patrolman/field training officer and detective with a metro Atlanta police department.
